Commercial Window Cleaning Contracts in BC: What to Know Before You Sign

Published 2026-05-30 · Wagon Windows

A recurring commercial window cleaning contract is one of the simplest operational decisions a business can make — and one of the most underused. Regular scheduling, a locked rate, and zero calendar management from you. Here's how they work, what's in a good one, and why Shuswap businesses that commit to contracts end up with consistently cleaner windows than those that don't.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Get an Instant Estimate (403) 837-5092

How Recurring Commercial Contracts Work

A commercial window cleaning contract is straightforward in concept: you agree to a regular schedule (weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly), a defined scope of work, and a fixed rate for a set term. In exchange, you get automatic scheduling with no call-in required, and we hold your rate for the contract period regardless of how our general pricing moves. The scheduling side matters more than people realize. The most common reason commercial windows don't get cleaned as often as they should is administrative friction — someone has to remember to call, get a quote, coordinate a time, and approve the invoice every single visit. For a busy restaurant manager, retail supervisor, or property manager, this process gets deprioritized constantly. A contract removes it entirely. The cleaning happens. You get the invoice. Done. Frequency options typically align with business type and traffic volume: - **Weekly**: High-touch entry glass, restaurants with significant fingerprint and grease exposure, retail in high-traffic tourist zones during peak season - **Bi-weekly**: General retail, service businesses, offices with public-facing windows, accommodations - **Monthly**: Lower-traffic commercial, professional services, businesses on quieter streets, shoulder-season scheduling for tourism businesses Some contracts use a seasonal model — monthly through fall and winter, bi-weekly or weekly during summer tourist season — which matches cleaning frequency to actual traffic and need. This is often the right structure for Shuswap businesses given how dramatically visitor volume changes between January and August.

  • Weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly frequency based on business type and traffic
  • Fixed rate locked for contract term — no price surprises mid-year
  • Automatic scheduling — no call-in required each visit
  • Seasonal frequency adjustments available for tourism-driven businesses

What Should Be in a Good Commercial Cleaning Contract

Not all commercial cleaning agreements are written the same way, and the quality of the document reflects the quality of the service relationship. Before signing any commercial window cleaning contract in BC, know what to look for. **Scope of work**: The contract should specify exactly which windows are included — exterior, interior, or both — and any exclusions (e.g., skylights, hard-to-reach upper-floor glass, leaded decorative windows). Ambiguity in scope leads to disputes later. If you have a specific window or section of glass you need included, name it in the contract. **Frequency and scheduling windows**: The contract should state the agreed frequency and whether visits are on a fixed day or within a scheduling window (e.g., 'bi-weekly, Tuesday-Thursday between 7am-10am'). If your business has times that don't work for cleaning — Saturday lunch rush at a restaurant, Monday morning at a busy retail store — these should be noted explicitly. **Rate and price escalation terms**: The base rate per visit should be stated. Understand whether the rate is fixed for the entire term or subject to annual adjustment, and if so, how much and with how much notice. **Term and cancellation**: Most commercial contracts run 6 or 12 months with 30-day written notice for cancellation outside of cause. Understand the exit terms before you sign. A good service provider will have straightforward cancellation terms — not penalty-heavy lock-ins. **Service standards and accountability**: What happens if a visit is missed? What's the process for flagging quality issues? A professional contract includes a service standard definition and a clear path for raising concerns.

  • Scope: specify which windows, interior/exterior, any exclusions
  • Scheduling: fixed day or window, blackout times during business peaks
  • Rate: base price per visit, escalation terms if any
  • Term: 6 or 12 months typical, 30-day cancellation notice standard
  • Service standards: missed visit policy and quality issue resolution process

Why Contracts Benefit Both Business and Cleaner

The contract model is genuinely mutual — it's not just about locking in a customer. Understanding the benefits to both parties explains why it produces better service. For the business owner, the benefits are operational consistency and price predictability. Windows get cleaned on schedule. The appearance standard is maintained without active management from you. You can build window cleaning into your operating budget as a fixed cost rather than a variable one-off. For restaurants and tourism businesses with seasonal revenue, knowing exactly what the window cleaning bill will be each month aids cash flow planning. For the window cleaner, a contract provides routing efficiency and predictable revenue. When multiple businesses on the same street or in the same commercial district are on contract, each visit becomes more efficient — travel time is amortized across multiple stops. This efficiency is what allows Wagon Windows to offer contract clients a better per-visit rate than the same service quoted as a one-off. The discount reflects real savings, not a promotional hook. This efficiency also means contract clients tend to get better scheduling priority. When a sudden opening appears in the calendar — a cancellation, a weather window — contract clients are more easily slotted in than one-time customers. Over time, this means contract clients get more consistent, timely service even during busy periods like the spring rush before the May long weekend. The relationship aspect matters too. A cleaner who visits a business monthly builds familiarity with the property — they know which windows are tricky, where the hose bib is, what the manager's preferences are. That institutional knowledge improves service quality over time in ways that a one-time-per-year call never achieves.

The cleanest windows in any commercial district belong to businesses on regular contracts. Not because one-time cleans are bad, but because consistency is what keeps glass presentable.

Shuswap Business Types That Benefit Most from Contracts

While any commercial business in the Shuswap can benefit from a window cleaning contract, certain types have the strongest case for regular scheduled service. **Restaurants and cafes**: Kitchen exhaust deposits grease on exterior glass, entry doors collect fingerprints daily, and patio windows see constant use through summer. The visual impact of restaurant windows on customer dining decisions is well-documented. Monthly at minimum; bi-weekly for busy season is the typical contract structure for food service in Salmon Arm. **Hotels and motels**: Accommodations on Shuswap Lake or the Trans-Canada through Salmon Arm and Sicamous serve guests whose first impression of the property happens through the parking lot. Window cleanliness is a direct signal of room quality. Properties that invest in exterior maintenance — including windows — consistently earn better online reviews and can support higher nightly rates. **Retail storefronts**: Particularly in tourist zones — Lakeshore Drive, downtown Salmon Arm, Sicamous main street — clean windows during peak season (May through September) are a competitive advantage. Bi-weekly contracts during tourist season, dropping to monthly in the shoulder months, is the typical structure. **Professional services**: Dentists, doctors, insurance brokers, banks — any practice where clients are evaluating trust through environmental cues benefits from consistent window maintenance. Monthly contracts typically fit this category. **Commercial property managers**: Multi-tenant buildings in Salmon Arm's expanding commercial areas benefit from property-level contracts that cover all units. This ensures consistent standards across tenants and simplifies billing for the property manager.

  • Restaurants: bi-weekly during summer, monthly in shoulder season
  • Hotels and accommodations: monthly or seasonal based on booking calendar
  • Tourism retail: bi-weekly May through September, monthly shoulder/winter
  • Professional services: monthly year-round
  • Commercial property managers: building-level contracts covering all units

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a commercial window cleaning contract cost in Salmon Arm?

Commercial storefronts start at $119 per visit. Contract pricing offers a better per-visit rate than one-off cleans, depending on frequency and scope. The exact rate depends on window count, accessibility, and whether interior cleaning is included. Call (403) 837-5092 to get a quote for your specific business — we'll assess the scope and give you a firm contract rate.

How long is a typical contract term?

Most commercial window cleaning contracts run six or twelve months. We don't believe in punitive lock-ins — if the service isn't working for you, the standard cancellation term is 30 days written notice. Our goal is to keep your business through quality, not through contract clauses.

Can we adjust the cleaning schedule seasonally for our tourism business?

Absolutely, and we recommend it. A common structure for Shuswap tourism businesses is bi-weekly visits from May through September, dropping to monthly from October through April. This matches cleaning frequency to actual visitor traffic and keeps your annual cost efficient. We can build seasonal frequency adjustments directly into the contract terms.

Do you serve commercial properties throughout the Shuswap or just Salmon Arm?

We serve businesses throughout the Shuswap region, including Salmon Arm, Sicamous, Sorrento, Armstrong, and surrounding areas. Commercial properties along the Trans-Canada corridor and in the Okanagan-Shuswap agricultural and tourism zones are within our service area. Call to confirm coverage for your location.
